title: potentilla × collina
intro: Potentilla × collina, also known as the sandy cinquefoil, is a perennial plant belonging to the Rosaceae family, which is a hybrid of Potentilla fruticosa and Potentilla argentea. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Potentilla × collina include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and even exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Potentilla × collina can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts in tinctures and salves.
